Title: Opposing effects of MYZUS PERSICAE-INDUCED LIPASE 1 and jasmonic acid influence the outcome of Arabidopsis thaliana–Fusarium graminearum interaction

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MPL1 promotes resistance to Fusarium graminearum by limiting jasmonic acid accumulation; overexpression decreases disease severity whereas knockout increases susceptibility.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: MPL1 is a candidate target to enhance Fusarium resistance by reducing JA-associated susceptibility; overexpression or activation may be useful, with caution because JA has broader roles in defence and development.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MPL1 encodes a lipase-like protein and is inferred to limit JA accumulation, but the direct biochemical mechanism is unresolved. Resistance conferred by MPL1 is independent of SA accumulation in the Fg pathosystem. MPL1 expression is induced by Fg infection and is highest in flowers/siliques among tested tissues.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Knockout mutants; overexpression lines; RT-qPCR; GUS reporter; hormone quantification; MeJA and ibuprofen pharmacology; double-mutant epistasis; disease scoring; fungal DNA quantification
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in flowers than rosette and cauline leaves; intermediate in inflorescence stem; strong in mature siliques and germinating seeds; induced by Fusarium graminearum in leaves, flowers, and siliques
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in flowers, inflorescence stem, siliques, and germinating seeds; induced upon Fusarium graminearum infection in inflorescence tissues and leaves
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Jasmonic acid is a signalling oxylipin; in this interaction elevated JA promotes Fusarium disease and can stimulate fungal germination/growth.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; methyl jasmonate pretreatment; ibuprofen treatment; salicylic acid treatment
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MPL1 expression is induced by Fusarium graminearum infection and is naturally high in reproductive tissues. 2. mpl1 loss-of-function mutants accumulate more JA and show greater Fusarium disease severity, while MPL1 overexpression lowers JA and enhances resistance. 3. MeJA suppresses MPL1-OE resistance and ibuprofen restores resistance in mpl1, demonstrating that MPL1 acts through limiting JA accumulation. 4. MPL1-mediated resistance is largely independent of SA, although MPL1-OE plants show elevated SA. 5. MPL1-OE plants also show enhanced callose deposition and ROS accumulation after infection.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential impacts on JA-dependent processes are possible but not directly tested; mechanism of JA limitation remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MPL1 promoter (inducible by Fusarium graminearum); overexpression promoter in MPL1-OE lines not stated
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Public-domain gene expression meta-analysis; no accession provided

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of OPR3 function (opr3-2), which blocks a JA biosynthesis step, enhanced resistance to Fusarium graminearum. 2. opr3-2 was epistatic to mpl1-1, and the mpl1-1 opr3-2 double mutant showed lower disease severity than mpl1-1, supporting that reduced JA biosynthesis limits susceptibility. 3. The paper uses OPR3 genetically to validate that MPL1 acts through modulation of jasmonic acid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OPR3 reduces jasmonic acid biosynthesis and promotes resistance to Fusarium graminearum.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 12-oxophytodienoic acid (OPDA) in the jasmonate biosynthetic pathway to enable JA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: OPC-8:0-related reduced intermediate leading to jasmonic acid
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing OPR3 activity could be used to lower JA accumulation and thereby decrease Fusarium susceptibility, but deployment should consider broad JA functions in defence and development.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible impairment of JA-dependent development and stress/insect defence; exact tradeoffs not evaluated in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defence and signaling metabolite; in this pathosystem elevated JA promotes Fusarium susceptibility and can stimulate fungal growth.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; double-mutant epistasis with mpl1-1; disease scoring
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway-validation gene in Results because epistasis directly establishes JA biosynthesis as causally linked to the mpl1 phenotype.
